author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
Mercy and Pity in the Judgment of God.
title: Mercy and Pity in the Judgment of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: This is the order observed in the psalm, where it is said: “Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all His recompenses; who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases; who redeemeth thy life from destruction; who crowneth thee with loving-kindness and tender mercy; who satisfieth thy desire with good things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And lest by any chance these great blessings should be despaired of under the deformity of our old, that is, mortal condition, the Psalmist at once says, “Thy youth shall be renewed like the eagle’s;” as much as to say, All that you have heard belongs to the new man and to the new covenant.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Now let us consider together briefly these things, and with delight contemplate the praise of mercy, that is, of the grace of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “Bless the Lord, O my soul,” he says, “and forget not all His recompenses.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Observe, he does not say blessings, but recompenses; because He recompenses evil with good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “Who forgiveth all thine iniquities:” this is done in the sacrament of baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “Who healeth all thy diseases:” this is effected by the believer in the present life, while the flesh so lusts against the spirit, and the spirit against the flesh, that we do not the things we would; whilst also another law in our members wars against the law of our mind; whilst to will is present indeed to us but not how to perform that which is good.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: These are the diseases of a man’s old nature which, however, if we only advance with persevering purpose, are healed by the growth of the new nature day by day, by the faith which operates through love.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “Who redeemeth thy life from destruction;” this will take place at the resurrection of the dead in the last day.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: “Who crowneth thee with loving-kindness and tender mercy;” this shall be accomplished in the day of judgment; for when the righteous King shall sit upon His throne to render to every man according to his works, who shall then boast of having a pure heart? or who shall glory of being clean from sin?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: It was therefore necessary to mention God’s loving-kindness and tender mercy there, where one might expect debts to be demanded and deserts recompensed so strictly as to leave no room for mercy.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: He crowns, therefore, with loving-kindness and tender mercy; but even so according to works.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: For he shall be separated to the right hand, to whom, it is said, “I was an hungered, and ye gave me meat.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: There will, however, be also “judgment without mercy;” but it will be for him “that hath not showed mercy.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: But “blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy” of God.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Then, as soon as those on the left hand shall have gone into eternal fire, the righteous, too, shall go into everlasting life, because He says: “This is life eternal, that they may know Thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And with this knowledge, this vision, this contemplation, shall the desire of their soul be satisfied; for it shall be enough for it to have this and nothing else,—there being nothing more for it to desire, to aspire to, or to require.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: It was with a craving after this full joy that his heart glowed who said to the Lord Christ, “Show us the Father, and it sufficeth us;” and to whom the answer was returned, “He that hath seen me hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: Because He is Himself the eternal life, in order that men may know the one true God, Thee and whom Thou hast sent, Jesus Christ.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: If, however, he that has seen the Son has also seen the Father, then assuredly he who sees the Father and the Son sees also the Holy Spirit of the Father and the Son.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: So we do not take away free will, whilst our soul blesses the Lord and forgets not all His recompenses; nor does it, in ignorance of God’s righteousness, wish to set up one of its own; but it believes in Him who justifies the ungodly, and until it arrives at sight, it lives by faith,—even the faith which works by love.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Spirit and the Letter.
verses.text: And this love is shed abroad in our hearts, not by the sufficiency of our own will, nor by the letter of the law, but by the Holy Ghost who has been given to us.
